chief_complaint: A 70-year-old woman was transferred to our hospital with a large mass in the right atrium.
history: Right atrial thrombus with pulmonary thromboembolism is a fatal condition. The patient was diagnosed with a giant floating thrombus in the right atrium complicated by pulmonary embolism.
exam: Vital signs were stable. Transthoracic echocardiography revealed a 16-x39-mm floating mass in the right atrium.
labs: Blood tests indicated disseminated intravascular coagulation (DIC). Contrast-enhanced computed tomography (CT) revealed thrombi in the bilateral pulmonary arteries, inferior vena cava, and right common iliac vein. A contrast defect in the right atrium was also observed, suggesting a thrombus.
correct_diagnosis: Giant floating thrombus in the right atrium complicated by pulmonary embolism
differentials: Giant floating thrombus in the right atrium complicated by pulmonary embolism
correct_ranking: Giant floating thrombus in the right atrium complicated by pulmonary embolism
